{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345","id":2615773672,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6b6Yno","number":56345,"title":"julia 1.11.1: typo causes hell to break loose","user":{"login":"gconte75","id":52205288,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUyMjA1Mjg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/52205288?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75","html_url":"https://github.com/gconte75","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-26T11:14:12Z","updated_at":"2024-10-26T11:14:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"On Linux, Debian Buster, Julia 1.11.1 (started as julia -p 4)

Today, I first typed:
  using Base.Threads
all good.

then, by mistake, I typed
 using Distributef        (instead of **_using Distributed_**)

instead of rejecting the typo, julia goes on hold for quite a while and then it gives an infinite list of errors related to a number of workers even though I haven't quite done anything.
Sometimes I can try and stop it by pressing ctrl-c or ctrl-x but on more than one occasion I had to kill the terminal window running julia.
Some other times it just crashes.

I have an older version of Julia (1.06 something I believe) on the same Linux machine that correctly rejects the typo with no further issues.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56345","id":2615773672,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6b6Yno","number":56345,"title":"julia 1.11.1: typo causes hell to break loose","user":{"login":"gconte75","id":52205288,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUyMjA1Mjg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/52205288?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75","html_url":"https://github.com/gconte75","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gconte75/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-26T11:14:12Z","updated_at":"2024-10-26T11:24:50Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"On Linux, Debian Buster, Julia 1.11.1 (started as julia -p 4)

Today, I first typed:
  using Base.Threads
all good.

then, by mistake, I typed
 using Distributef        (instead of **_using Distributed_**)

instead of rejecting the typo, julia goes on hold for quite a while and then it gives an infinite list of errors related to a number of workers even though I haven't quite done anything.
Sometimes I can try and stop it by pressing ctrl-c or ctrl-x but on more than one occasion I had to kill the terminal window running julia.
Some other times it just crashes.

I have an older version of Julia (1.06 something I believe) on the same Linux machine that correctly rejects the typo with no further issues.

I just realized that the -p command line parameter implicitly causes the Distributed package to load into julia, so the _using Distributed_ command is redundant. 
Still, it looks like a bug.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2439526226/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
